title: TotpEnrollmentPreview
description: DTO returned by POST /api/v1/me/mfa/totp/enroll/preview (round 1 of the two-round TOTP enrolment). Emits the plaintext TOTP secret ONCE — the caller must round-trip it back to the server on round 2 (POST /enroll) with a valid confirmation code to complete enrolment. If round 2 never runs, PurgeUnconfirmedEnrollmentsJob evicts the ephemeral state (Redis, 5-minute TTL). Not persisted to Postgres — this is a signed envelope response, not a durable row.

x-storage.encryption_note: The envelope carries the plaintext TOTP secret. Redis MUST have at-rest encryption enabled OR the deployment MUST configure `mfa.totp.preview_backend='session'` to fall back to Laravel's encrypted session driver.

totp_secret.description: Base32-encoded shared secret. 32 characters (encodes 20 bytes = 160 bits of entropy per RFC 6238 recommendation). Emitted ONCE for QR-code generation client-side. Round 2 confirms by submitting the secret + a valid TOTP code — server verifies against the ephemeral envelope, not against a database row. Never emitted after round 2 confirmation.

totp_uri.description: otpauth:// URI encoding the secret + issuer + label. Format: `otpauth://totp/{issuer}:{account_label}?secret={base32_secret}&issuer={issuer}&digits=6&period=30&algorithm=SHA1`. Rendered as a QR code by the SPA. account_label is the email fingerprint (SHA-256 truncated), NEVER the plaintext email — protects the caller if the QR code is photographed.

issuer.description: Human-readable issuer string that appears in the authenticator app's account list (e.g. 'Academorix'). Tenant admins can override via config('mfa.totp.issuer') settings; caller-facing string only, no security material.

account_label.description: Label shown next to the issuer in the authenticator app. SHA-256 truncated fingerprint of the caller's email — NEVER the plaintext email. Preserves privacy if the QR code is photographed / shared.

expires_at.description: When the ephemeral envelope evicts from Redis. Caller must confirm within this window. Typically 5 minutes from issuance.

preview_id.description: Ephemeral ID of the Redis envelope (used to correlate MfaEnrollmentPreviewIssued → MfaEnrollmentConfirmed events). NOT a durable row identifier; the server-side envelope is keyed on session, not preview_id.

x-wire.cache_control_reason: Response carries plaintext secret; must not be cached by proxies / browser HTTP cache. Explicit `Cache-Control: no-store` header on the response.

invariants: The plaintext TOTP secret exists ONLY inside a signed Redis envelope + the response body of this endpoint. It is NEVER persisted to Postgres before round-2 confirmation. It is NEVER logged. It is NEVER included in metrics labels. It is NEVER echoed in error messages.

The endpoint refuses to issue a preview when the caller's Identity already has a TOTP secret persisted (`Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted IS NOT NULL`) — with error MFA_ALREADY_ENROLLED (409). To rotate, disable first, then re-enroll.

Repeated calls within the 5-minute window return the SAME envelope + secret — the endpoint is idempotent by session ID. Reissuing a fresh secret on every call would defeat the two-round protocol (a slow user might scan an old secret + submit a fresh code that doesn't match the fresh secret).
